title: Mounted file changes are not detected  by Rails or NPM inside the containers
body:
### Issue Description

We have found an issue when migrating from Docker to Podman that we have not been able to fix yet.

When we mount files to the container on our local setup, neither Rails nor Gulp.js are picking the changes to the files made from the host.
When we look inside the container, the files are correctly changed.

I have not tried many tools but `Rails` server is not picking up the changes, neither `Gulp.js` and `Vite.js`. 
With `vite.js` I was able to make it work by adding `usePolling: true` in the configuration, but for the other we don't have a fix. 

I created a [repository](https://github.com/AshDevFr/podman-fs-events-test) with an example to see the difference between Docker and Podman behavior.

---

When editing a mounted file directly from within the container instead of from the host, the change is detected. 

---

I have tried to play with `--userns=keep-id`but it did not fix the behavior.

I also tried the following that I found [here](https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/issues/654#issuecomment-2135644779)

```yaml
    environment:
      PODMAN_USERNS: "keep-id:uid=${MY_UID}:1000,gid=${MY_GID}:1000" # Define in .env
```

The issue persist. 

---

```bash
podman version 5.2.5
Running on MacOS (Arm64)
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Clone the repository [https://github.com/AshDevFr/podman-fs-events-test](https://github.com/AshDevFr/podman-fs-events-test)
2. Use the `podman` or `podman compose` commands from the README
3. Change a JS file and see that the change is not picked up by `vite.js`


### Describe the results you received

When using podman, the file changes are not detected by our tools running in the container.

### Describe the results you expected

It should work like Docker and our tools should be able to detect the changes
